title: Resolution is upsert-by-normalized-ID, not a merge pass
body: Cross-source entity resolution falls out of giving each mention a normalized ID (normalize.py) and letting Graph.upsert_node union sources/props on collision. There is no separate matching step. When adding an entity kind or a new source, the only resolution work is choosing the ID function — get that right and merges are automatic and narratable. The alias table (aliases.yaml) is the sole non-mechanical step.

title: Traverse over neighbors(), not openCypher, to keep backends identical
body: Multi-hop traversal runs in query.py over GraphStore.neighbors(), so MemoryGraphStore and NeptuneGraphStore produce a byte-identical trace and the whole graph path is testable without a live cluster. Pushing traversal into openCypher would diverge the backends and break the offline demo/test story — it is an explicit Boundaries rail, deferred to slice 3.

title: openCypher can't parameterize relationship types — use one REL type + a kind param
body: Relationship types are not parameterizable in openCypher, so the naive edge query interpolates the kind into the string (an injection vector). Model every edge as a single REL type carrying a `kind` property; then MATCH (a)-[r:REL {kind: $kind}]->(b) keeps kind a bound parameter. Same trick keeps node ids parameterized via a single Entity label.

title: Always yaml.safe_load — the corpus is untrusted input parsed under an IAM role
body: The Fargate task parses external repo YAML while holding an S3+Neptune+STS task role, so a !!python/object tag under yaml.load would be RCE in the VPC (CWE-502). Use yaml.safe_load everywhere; the ruff S ruleset (S506) catches a regression. A negative fixture (tests/fixtures/unsafe.yaml) asserts the tag parses inert.

title: CDK synth tests: least-privilege check must exclude ecr:GetAuthorizationToken
body: Asserting 'no IAM statement has Resource "*"' fails on a legitimate AWS requirement: ecr:GetAuthorizationToken on the execution role must be "*". Scope the least-privilege assertion to the task role's app actions (neptune-db:connect, s3 read) instead. Also: set JSII_SILENCE_WARNING_UNTESTED_NODE_VERSION so the node-version warning doesn't clutter output; Template.from_stack synthesizes in-process with no cdk CLI or AWS account.

title: Neptune DB subnet group requires >=2 AZs — synth passes, deploy fails
body: A Neptune (RDS-family) DB subnet group must span >=2 Availability Zones or `cdk deploy` fails at CloudFormation time with a subnet-group error. `Template.from_stack` synth assertions do NOT catch this — it's a deploy-time rule. Set VPC max_azs>=2 even for a single-instance serverless cluster (subnets are free; only running compute costs, so it doesn't break a single-AZ cost posture). Always run the real `cdk synth` CLI AND assert >=2 SubnetIds in the synth test; better, deploy once to a throwaway account.

title: AWS_REGION is a reserved ECS variable — don't set it in a task-def environment
body: ECS/Fargate injects AWS_REGION/AWS_DEFAULT_REGION into every container from the task metadata; re-declaring AWS_REGION in the task definition's `environment` is redundant and some agent versions reject it. Let the agent provide it and let botocore's default chain read it. Only bake non-secret, non-reserved config (endpoints, bucket names) into task-def env; credentials always come from the task role via the botocore chain, never plaintext env.

title: EC2 SG group AND rule descriptions must use a restricted ASCII charset
body: `cdk synth` does not validate security-group descriptions, but EC2 rejects them at deploy time. GroupDescription rejects non-ASCII (an em-dash failed). Ingress-rule Description is even stricter: allowed set is a-zA-Z0-9 plus . _-:/()#,@[]+=&;{}!$* and space -- notably NO '>' (so 'ingestion -> neptune' fails). Two separate failed deploys taught this. Guarded now by test_security_group_descriptions_use_ec2_charset (only validates literal strings; CDK auto-generates some rule descriptions as Fn::Join intrinsics which are valid). Always run the real `cdk synth` CLI and, ideally, one throwaway deploy.

title: Cache AWS creds once per session; let cdk block instead of polling
body: A per-command credential_process / SSO provider gets rate-limited ('auth limit') when every aws/cdk call re-resolves credentials. Resolve once with `aws configure export-credentials --format env` into a mode-600 session file and source it for all later calls (see scripts/_aws-env.sh; refresh at >50min). And don't tight-poll describe-stacks: `cdk deploy`/`destroy` already block until CloudFormation settles -- run them in the background and wait for completion, use a single status.sh check if needed (never a fast loop).

title: VPC OpenSearch domain applies access_policies via an AwsCustomResource, not the inline property
body: For a VPC-resident opensearchservice.Domain, CDK does NOT render the access policy as the AWS::OpenSearchService::Domain `AccessPolicies` property — it provisions a Custom::AWS (AwsCustomResource) calling updateDomainConfig, with the policy JSON embedded (escaped) in its Create/Update parameters. A synth test that reads domain['Properties']['AccessPolicies'] KeyErrors. Assert against the serialized whole-template JSON instead (es:ESHttp*, the scoped domain ARN, the named role logical-ids, and the absence of an AllPrincipals wildcard).

title: Avoid an OpenSearch access-policy <-> role dependency cycle with a computed ARN + fixed domain name
body: If the domain access policy references role ARNs AND the roles' policies reference domain.domain_arn, CDK detects a cycle. Break it: pin a fixed domain_name and compute the domain ARN via self.format_arn(service='es', resource='domain', resource_name=f'{name}/*') as a plain string in both the access policy and the role policies (no live domain.domain_arn ref), and create the roles before the domain so the access policy can name their ARNs. Single-node domains also need exactly ONE subnet (zone awareness off) or synth/deploy errors.

title: Make an embedding-quality eval honest AND reproducible with frozen real vectors
body: A credible vector-baseline eval must reflect real embedding quality, but CI can't call Bedrock deterministically. Resolve it by embedding the curated query set + corpus once with real Titan v2 and committing the vectors as a fixture (frozen_embeddings.json); the eval runs cosine k-NN over the frozen vectors offline and asserts hit@5. A --bedrock --refresh-embeddings CLI path regenerates them; the live OpenSearch+Bedrock round-trip is proven separately by the in-VPC probe. Entity-led 'misses' are realizable because the approver/owner edge lives in kep.yaml (not chunked), so no chunk carries the @handle the query names.

title: mypy aborts on numpy 3.12-syntax stubs under a 3.11 target — skip-follow numpy
body: On a py3.13 interpreter with numpy>=2.x installed transitively (pandas/docling/etc.) and mypy python_version=3.11, mypy reaches numpy's stubs by following installed aws_cdk deps and dies on 'Type statement is only supported in Python 3.12+', aborting before checking any project code. numpy is NOT a project dep. Fix in pyproject: a [[tool.mypy.overrides]] for module=['numpy','numpy.*'] with follow_imports='skip' + ignore_missing_imports=true. ignore_missing_imports alone does NOT help (the stub is found, just unparseable).

title: One service-name constant feeds both SigV4 signing and the IAM action prefix
body: The OpenSearch adapter signs SigV4 for service 'es' and the IaC grants es:ESHttp*; if those drift, requests fail only live (AuthorizationException). Define OPENSEARCH_SERVICE='es' once in store/opensearch.py and reference the same 'es' in the IaC action list, so a future edit can't change one without the other. Same lesson as the Neptune adapter's NEPTUNE_SERVICE.

title: A VPC OpenSearch domain needs the OpenSearch service-linked role pre-created, or create fails
body: Creating an opensearchservice.Domain inside a VPC fails at CloudFormation time with 'Before you can proceed, you must enable a service-linked role to give Amazon OpenSearch Service permissions to access your VPC.' CDK does NOT create the SLR. Create it once per account (idempotent): aws iam create-service-linked-role --aws-service-name opensearchservice.amazonaws.com (and es.amazonaws.com for the legacy es-engine VPC access). deploy.sh now does this before cdk deploy so 'one-command deploy on a clean account' holds. Synth tests can't catch it (deploy-time rule). A failed CREATE leaves the stack in ROLLBACK_COMPLETE which must be deleted before re-deploy (cdk deploy auto-deletes it).

title: grant_invoke_url with a CfnParameter principal fails synth (token in construct ID)
body: fn.grant_invoke_url(iam.ArnPrincipal(cfn_param.value_as_string)) raises 'ComponentsContainUnresolvedTokens: ID components may not include unresolved tokens' at synth: the grant derives a construct ID from the principal ARN, and a CfnParameter is an unresolved token. Fix: create the permission explicitly with lambda_.CfnPermission(action='lambda:InvokeFunctionUrl', function_name=fn.function_arn, principal=param.value_as_string, function_url_auth_type='AWS_IAM') — it takes the token as the Principal directly. add_function_url(auth_type=AWS_IAM) still creates the URL.

title: Scope a Bedrock cross-region inference profile to BOTH the profile ARN and the regional foundation-model ARNs
body: A 'us.anthropic.*' inference profile is invoked by the profile id but Bedrock authorizes against the underlying regional foundation models it routes to. A grant scoped only to the inference-profile ARN fails live with AccessDenied on the foundation model. Scope both: arn:aws:bedrock:<region>:<account>:inference-profile/us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6 AND arn:aws:bedrock:<each-region>::foundation-model/anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6 (foundation-model ARNs carry no account id). Grant bedrock:InvokeModel + bedrock:Converse; never a wildcard Resource.

title: Keep PyYAML out of the pure-Python Lambda import graph; entity-link with aliases={} in the handler
body: query_lambda.py is bundled via Code.from_asset over the package source with boto3/botocore from the runtime but NO pyyaml. Its transitive imports must not load yaml. resolve.load_aliases() (display-name->handle table) uses yaml, so the live handler runs link_question(question, aliases={}): the mechanical normalizers (@handles, SIG slugs, KEP numbers) still resolve; only bare display names need the table, and the curated demo questions don't use them. hybrid/synthesize/entity_link/compare import only model/store/vector/embed (yaml-free). load_showcase also uses yaml — keep it CLI/test-only, never imported by the Lambda. Guard with a test that blocks `import yaml` and imports query_lambda.

title: Sign a Function-URL POST so the signature covers the body (X-Amz-Content-SHA256, not UNSIGNED-PAYLOAD)
body: botocore SigV4Auth.add_auth computes the payload hash into the canonical request but does NOT emit an X-Amz-Content-SHA256 header by default. To make a tampered body provably rejected, set X-Amz-Content-SHA256 = sha256(body).hexdigest() as a header BEFORE add_auth so it becomes a signed header. Sign service='lambda' for a Function URL; extract the signing region from the host (<id>.lambda-url.<region>.on.aws). Raise loudly on non-2xx with the body on the CLI side.

title: expand_neighborhood is O(frontier x 6 edge-kinds x 2 dirs) sequential Neptune calls — fine in-memory, times out live
body: The app-layer expansion over neighbors() (kept that way for backend-identical traces) issues one openCypher HTTP round-trip per (node, edge_kind, direction). Against the in-memory store that is instant, so offline tests pass; against Neptune Serverless at minimum NCU each query is ~300ms, so a 2-hop expansion over a small frontier becomes hundreds of sequential SigV4+HTTPS queries and the query Lambda hit its 120s timeout on every live invocation (CloudWatch Duration max=120000ms; no fast error). Live-only finding (2026-06-24). Fix: add a batched neighbor fetch to GraphStore (one openCypher query per hop for the whole frontier, all_edges()-shaped, parameterized $ids) with a default app-layer fan-out so MemoryGraphStore + the trace stay identical; and give the CLI --function-url client a longer, configurable read timeout (it inherits the Neptune adapter's hard-coded 30s). See docs/backlog.md hybrid-orchestration-live-deploy.

title: In-VPC compute SGs must allow_all_outbound; a closed SG silently hangs the first Bedrock call to the timeout
body: The query Lambda timed out at its 120s budget with ZERO application logs on every live invocation. Root cause: its SG (QuerySg) was created allow_all_outbound=False (copying the store-SG pattern), so the in-VPC compute could not initiate ANY outbound — and its first call (boto3 Bedrock Titan embed, default 60s connect x retries) hung to the budget, never reaching Neptune/OpenSearch (whose adapters have 30s timeouts that would have errored fast and logged). The fix: in-VPC COMPUTE SGs (the query Lambda, Fargate task, smoke probes) use allow_all_outbound=True; the 'no-egress-path' guarantee is the no-NAT topology (egress can only reach VPC endpoints + in-VPC stores, no internet), NOT a closed SG. Only the store/endpoint SGs (Neptune, OpenSearch) use allow_all_outbound=False. Tell from the symptom: a 120s timeout with no app logs + Duration==timeout = stuck in a no-30s-timeout call (boto3 Bedrock); a 30s-ish error with a logged exception = a store adapter hop. Guarded by test_query_lambda_sg_allows_outbound (synth: the SG has a 0.0.0.0/0 protocol -1 egress). Live finding 2026-06-24.

title: Injectable HttpClient test fakes must match the real urllib client's raise-vs-return contract on 4xx/5xx
body: The store adapters' HttpClient Protocol contract is return-not-raise: _request does a uniform `if not 200 <= status < 300: raise RuntimeError(...)` check, so the client must RETURN an HttpResponse on a 4xx/5xx, not raise. The test fakes (RecordingHttp) honoured this — they pop a prepared HttpResponse(400, ...) — but the real default _UrllibClient did NOT: urllib.request.urlopen RAISES urllib.error.HTTPError on any 4xx/5xx, so the real client violated the contract the fakes encoded. Because HTTPError is an OSError subclass (not RuntimeError), it sailed past both _request's status check AND OpenSearchVectorStore.create_index's `except RuntimeError` already-exists tolerance, so the Fargate ingestion aborted with an uncaught HTTPError 400 whenever the index already existed (slice-2 probe leaves it behind). Latent because every unit test used the lenient fake. Fix: catch HTTPError ONLY (never the broader URLError, or TLS/connection failures get swallowed into a fabricated response) and return HttpResponse(status=e.code, text=e.read().decode('utf-8', errors='replace')). Lesson: when a seam has an injectable fake, the fake's raise/return behaviour IS part of the contract — add a unit test that exercises the REAL default impl against the same error shape, or the fake silently diverges. store/neptune.py's _UrllibClient still has the identical latent bug (deferred: docs/backlog.md neptune-urllib-http-error-idempotency). Live finding 2026-06-24.

title: Permission-filtering a graph traversal by post-filtering the final node set leaks via reachability
body: When a retrieval graph carries access labels, filtering only the FINAL result set (drop forbidden nodes after traversal) is a leak: a forbidden node still enters the frontier, still appears in the hop trace, and can still bridge to a node reachable ONLY through it (seed -> forbidden R -> B; post-filter drops R but still surfaces B, which is only known via the forbidden path). The filter must be applied DURING traversal, on the edge, so a forbidden node never enters the frontier at all. In this repo the predicate is `edge.visibility IN allowed AND neighbor.visibility IN allowed`, applied in GraphStore.neighbors/neighbors_batch (in-memory: where it iterates edges; Neptune: a parameterized WHERE on the openCypher hop). Because edge.visibility = compose(src,dst) = max and a clearance is downward-closed, the edge check IS the node guarantee. The regression test is a restricted-intermediate fixture a post-filter would fail (test_query.py::test_expand_edge_filter_no_leak_for_public_reader). Note: this is a SYNTHETIC teaching stand-in for ACLs, not real authz (charter principle 5).

title: Compose derived visibility labels most-restrictive-wins (max); keep the read path PyYAML-free
body: Two patterns from the permission-filter slice. (1) A derived label is the MAX (most-restrictive) of its inputs: an edge's visibility = compose(src,dst); a chunk's = compose(owning entity ids); unlabeled defaults to the least-restrictive tier (public). This makes 'the edge is traversable' equivalent to 'both endpoints are visible' given a downward-closed clearance, so one rule (the edge filter) gives the node guarantee with no second mechanism. (2) Split the label lifecycle across the PyYAML boundary: label ASSIGNMENT (reads labels.yaml) lives in graphrag.labels and runs only on the ingest path (Fargate/CLI, where pyyaml is available); the query-time CLEARANCE resolution (persona -> allowed tiers) lives in graphrag.visibility as pure-Python constants with NO yaml import, so the in-VPC query Lambda (PyYAML-free Code.from_asset bundle) imports it freely. By query time the labels are already baked into the stores' node/edge/chunk properties, so the read path needs no yaml. Guarded by a sys.modules assertion that importing query_lambda never pulls in graphrag.labels.

title: A new packaged resource (*.yaml) must be added to [tool.setuptools.package-data] or pip install . / the Fargate image omits it
body: src-layout offline tests (pytest with rootdir on packages/graphrag/src) resolve importlib.resources paths against the SOURCE tree, so load_labels()/load_aliases()/load_showcase() pass even when the file is NOT declared in [tool.setuptools.package-data]. But `pip install .` (the Fargate ingestion Dockerfile) only bundles the files listed there, so a forgotten entry ships an image whose load_*() crashes with FileNotFoundError at runtime — a live-only failure the whole offline suite + cdk synth miss. Slice-4 hit this: labels.yaml was in src but absent from package-data (which listed aliases.yaml, py.typed, showcase/queries.yaml). Symptom: Fargate task or query Lambda raises FileNotFoundError on '/usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/graphrag/<file>.yaml'. Fix: add the file to package-data. Guard: test_labels.py::test_all_packaged_yaml_declared_in_package_data parses pyproject and asserts every *.yaml under src/graphrag is declared — catches the next forgotten resource at unit time. Note the query Lambda uses Code.from_asset (zips the src tree as-is, so it does NOT hit this — only the pip-install-. Fargate image does). Same live-only class as opensearch-create-index-idempotency.

title: Dual-form (app-layer vs Neptune) identity tests must use independent ground truth, not derive the mock from the thing under test
body: The repo runs traversal/queries in two forms that must return byte-identical sorted output: an app-layer evaluator over the GraphStore seam (in-memory/offline) and a parameterized openCypher form (live Neptune) — the neighbors_batch invariant, and (opencypher-templates) each Cypher Template's evaluate vs its cypher. When unit-testing that identity OFFLINE you cannot run the real cypher, so you feed a mocked Neptune HTTP response. The trap: if you build that mock response FROM the app-layer result (e.g. `neptune_rows = [{...id: n.id} for n in reversed(app_rows)]`), the assertion `neptune_ids == app_ids` is vacuous — the mock just echoes the app side back, so a real evaluator-vs-cypher divergence passes green. Fix: carry an INDEPENDENT hardcoded `expected` per case and assert BOTH sides == expected (parametrize over every template/edge-shape, since the divergence-prone ones are incoming edges, two-edge-kind + node-kind-filter arms, and inbound). The app-layer side stays anchored to known-correct rows; the cypher's real result semantics are verified live (the deferred live AC). Caught by the adversarial-reviewer on opencypher-templates after a first 'parametrize it' fix made it tautological.
source: docs/specs/opencypher-templates/; packages/graphrag/AGENTS.md (Cypher Templates dual-form invariant); test_templates_exec.py
